LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTERCONTINENTAL HOTELS GROUP RESOURCES, INC.'S MOTION REQUIRING PLAINTIFF JULIE CALAKI TO FILE AN UNDERTAKING TO SECURE AN AWARD OF FEES AND COSTS. DEFENDANT (AN INTERNATIONAL HOTEL CHAIN) MOVES UNDER CCP 1030 TO FORCE PLAINTIFF (A HOTEL GUEST WHO ALLEGES SHE SLIPPED AND FELL ON AN OILY SPOT) TO FILE A $36,905.55 UNDERTAKING IN ORDER TO MAINTAIN HER SUIT. THE MOTION IS DENIED. "THE PURPOSE OF SECTION 1030 IS TO PROTECT CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS WHO ARE SUED BY OUT-OF-STATE PLAINTIFFS WHEN THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THE OUT-OF-STATE PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL." (YAO V. SUP. CT. (2002) 104 CAL.APP.4TH 327, 333.) DEFENDANT (INCORPORATED IN DELAWARE) IS NOT A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT. NOR DOES IT DEMONSTRATE "NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY" PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL.(SEE ID.)INDEED, DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS ARE CONTRADICTORY - IT FIRST CONTENDS THAT THE OILY SPOT WAS NOT OPEN AND OBVIOUS AND NEXT THAT IT WAS. COUNSEL FOR MS. CALAKI IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Continued To Oct-21-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Requiring Plaintiff Julie Calaki To File An Undertaking To Secure An Award Of Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT INTERCONTINENTAL HOTELS GROUP RESOURCES, INC.'S Motion Requiring Plaintiff Julie Calaki To File An Undertaking To Secure An Award Of Fees And Costs.

Defendant (an international hotel chain) moves under CCP 1030 to force plaintiff (a hotel guest who alleges she slipped and fell on an oily spot) to file a $36,905.55 undertaking in order to maintain her suit. The motion is DENIED. "The purpose of section 1030 is to protect California residents who are sued by out-of-state plaintiffs when there is no reasonable possibility the out-of-state plaintiff will prevail." (Yao v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 327, 333.) Defendant (incorporated in Delaware) is not a California resident. Nor does it demonstrate "no reasonable possibility" plaintiff will prevail. (See id.) Indeed, defendant's arguments are contradictory - it first contends that the oily spot was not open and obvious and next that it was.

Counsel for Ms. Calaki is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
